missing.explore_drag_the_protractor_name_the_a: Explore — drag the protractor, name the angle
missing.acute_90_right_90_obtuse_90_180_straig: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse 90–180°, straight = 180°, reflex > 180°. The protractor was invented around 1801; the half-circle version still runs every geometry class.
missing.estimation_builds_spatial_sense_precis: Estimation builds spatial sense; precise measurement builds the protractor habit. Common Core 4.MD.5–7: angles as fractions of a full turn (1/360 of a rotation).
missing.complementary_sum_to_90_supplementary_: Complementary sum to 90°; supplementary to 180°; vertical pairs are equal; alternate interior angles match when lines are parallel. These four facts unlock most middle-school proofs.
missing.bisect_cut_in_half_the_construction_wi: Bisect = cut in half (the construction with compass + straightedge is in Euclid Book I, Proposition 9). Modern tools: protractor, miter saw, theodolite — all the same idea, calibrated.
missing.click_to_get_personalized_angle_tips: Click to get personalized angle tips!
missing.click_to_get_personalized_angle_tips_2: Click to get personalized angle tips!
missing.what_type_of_angle_is_this: What type of angle is this?
missing.look_at_the_angle_on_the_protractor_nu: Look at the angle on the protractor (number hidden). How many degrees is it?
missing.opposite_angles_formed_by_intersecting: Opposite angles formed by intersecting lines; always equal
missing.angles_that_share_a_common_vertex_and_: Angles that share a common vertex and side
missing.two_rays_overlap_exactly_no_rotation: Two rays overlap exactly. No rotation.
missing.less_than_a_right_angle_pizza_slice_le: Less than a right angle. Pizza slice, "less than square".
missing.exactly_90_corner_of_a_square_perpendi: Exactly 90°. Corner of a square. Perpendicular lines.
missing.between_right_and_straight_wider_than_: Between right and straight. Wider than square corner.
missing.half_rotation_the_two_rays_form_a_stra: Half rotation. The two rays form a straight line.
missing.greater_than_a_straight_angle_measure_: Greater than a straight angle. Measure the "outside" of the angle.
missing.complete_turn_the_two_rays_overlap_exa: Complete turn. The two rays overlap exactly (like 0°, but went all the way around).
missing.interactive_geometry_references_pick_a: Interactive geometry references — pick a topic.
missing.interior_angle_sum_n_2_180_for_any_n_s: Interior angle sum = (n − 2) × 180° for any n-sided polygon. Each exterior angle of a REGULAR polygon = 360°/n. Sum of exterior angles always = 360°.
missing.soh_cah_toa_sine_opp_hyp_cosine_adj_hy: SOH-CAH-TOA: Sine = Opp/Hyp, Cosine = Adj/Hyp, Tangent = Opp/Adj. The trig functions express ratios of right-triangle sides; extended to all angles via the unit circle.
missing.these_angles_their_trig_values_are_wor: These angles + their trig values are worth memorizing — they appear constantly in math, physics, and engineering.
missing.1_rad_180_57_296_1_180_0_01745_rad_1_g: 1 rad = 180/π ≈ 57.296°. 1° = π/180 ≈ 0.01745 rad. 1 gon = 0.9°. Most calculators have a degrees/radians mode toggle — make sure you're in the right mode!
missing.bearings_measured_clockwise_from_north: Bearings measured CLOCKWISE from north. Standard 16-point compass has 22.5° between adjacent directions.
missing.three_views_of_the_same_trigonometric_: Three views of the SAME trigonometric setup. Drag the angle (or tower height) slider — every representation updates simultaneously. Then take the inverse-problem quiz: solve a real measurement from only one representation.
missing.change_the_angle_and_watch_all_three_u: Change the angle and watch all three update together — this is what "the same idea, three representations" means.
missing.pisa_is_roughly_56_m_tall_leaning_4_to: (Pisa is roughly 56 m tall, leaning ≈ 4° today)
missing.each_quiz_gives_you_partial_info_use_t: Each quiz gives you partial info. Use the relationships you discovered above to compute the missing value.
missing.tip_rearrange_tan_opp_adj_if_you_know_: Tip: rearrange tan(θ) = opp/adj — if you know θ and one side, you can solve for the other. If you know both sides, take arctan.
missing.quantity_with_both_magnitude_and_direc: Quantity with both magnitude AND direction. Drawn as arrow. Examples: velocity, force, displacement.
missing.scalar_just_a_number_mass_temperature_: Scalar = just a number (mass, temperature, time). Vector = magnitude + direction.
missing.place_tail_of_second_at_head_of_first_: Place tail of second at head of first. Resultant = first tail to second head.
missing.place_tails_together_diagonal_of_paral: Place tails together. Diagonal of parallelogram = resultant.
missing.vector_v_at_angle_v_v_cos_v_y_v_sin: Vector v at angle θ: vₓ = v·cos θ, v_y = v·sin θ.
missing.v_v_v_y_v_z_pythagoras_in_any_dimensio: |v| = √(vₓ² + v_y² + v_z²). Pythagoras in any dimension.
missing.magnitude_1_v_v_v_often_called_the_dir: Magnitude 1. v̂ = v / |v|. Often called the "direction" of v.
missing.a_b_a_b_cos_gives_scalar_zero_when_per: a·b = |a||b|cos θ. Gives scalar. Zero when perpendicular. Negative when angle > 90°.
missing.a_b_a_b_sin_n_gives_vector_perpendicul: a×b = |a||b|sin θ · n̂. Gives vector PERPENDICULAR to both. Magnitude = area of parallelogram.
missing.curl_fingers_from_a_to_b_thumb_points_: Curl fingers from a to b. Thumb points in direction of a×b. (Right-handed coordinates.)
missing.c_a_c_b_generates_a_plane_or_line_of_v: c₁·a + c₂·b. Generates a plane (or line) of vectors.
missing.newton_s_2nd_law_f_ma_net_force_is_vec: Newton's 2nd law: F = ma. Net force is vector sum of all forces.
missing.airplane_heading_wind_vector_ground_ve: Airplane heading + wind vector → ground velocity (vector sum).
missing.equilateral_triangle_square_regular_he: Equilateral triangle, square, regular hexagon. Only regular polygons that tile the plane.
missing.interior_angle_must_divide_360_triangl: Interior angle must divide 360°. Triangle 60° (×6), square 90° (×4), hexagon 120° (×3). Pentagon 108° doesn't divide 360° → can't tile alone.
missing.8_in_total_combine_multiple_regular_po: 8 in total. Combine multiple regular polygons; same arrangement at each vertex.
missing.aperiodic_tiling_never_repeats_exactly: Aperiodic tiling — never repeats exactly. 5-fold symmetry. Inspired discovery of quasicrystals.
missing.hexagonal_tiling_minimizes_total_perim: Hexagonal tiling minimizes total perimeter for given area. Proven by Hales (1999). Why bees use hexagons.
missing.each_region_points_closest_to_one_seed: Each region = points closest to one "seed" point. Appears in nature (giraffe spots, mud cracks, cell organization).
missing.on_a_hyperbolic_plane_regular_pentagon: On a hyperbolic plane, regular pentagons (and many other shapes) CAN tile. M.C. Escher's "Circle Limit" series.
missing.colored_squares_that_must_match_neighb: Colored squares that must match neighbors on edges. Can simulate Turing machine → tiling problem is undecidable in general.
missing.a_single_tile_that_aperiodically_tiles: A single tile that aperiodically tiles the plane (the "hat" and "spectre" shapes). Major recent discovery.
missing.in_a_right_triangle_a_b_c_where_c_is_t: In a right triangle, a² + b² = c² where c is the hypotenuse. Sets of integers (a, b, c) that satisfy this are called Pythagorean triples. A "primitive" triple has no common factor.
missing.for_positive_integers_m_n_with_no_comm: For positive integers m > n with no common factor and not both odd, a = m² − n², b = 2mn, c = m² + n². Tries m=2, n=1 → (3,4,5).
missing.careers_that_use_angles_every_day: 💼 Careers that use angles every day
missing.circle_has_most_area_for_a_perimeter: Circle has most area for a perimeter
missing.isoperimetric_inequality_4_a_p_equalit: Isoperimetric inequality: 4πA ≤ P². Equality only for circles. Why soap bubbles + planets are round.
missing.sphere_has_most_volume_for_a_surface_a: Sphere has most volume for a surface area
missing.3d_version_of_above_single_bubbles_are: 3D version of above. Single bubbles are spheres; merged bubbles meet at 120° angles.
missing.honeycomb_conjecture_proven_1999_by_ha: Honeycomb conjecture (proven 1999 by Hales): hexagonal tiling has lowest total edge length per area among any partition.
missing.cannot_be_deformed_without_changing_si: Cannot be deformed without changing side lengths. Why bridges + roof trusses use triangles.
missing.square_tiles_fill_the_plane_with_no_ga: Square tiles fill the plane with no gaps. Same for rectangles + parallelograms.
missing.hexagonal_packing_of_circles_fills_90_: Hexagonal packing of circles fills ~90.7% of plane (best possible). Square packing only ~78.5%.
missing.fcc_hcp_both_achieve_74_density_kepler: FCC + HCP both achieve ~74% density (Kepler conjecture, proven 2014 by Hales).
missing.only_5_in_3d_tetrahedron_cube_octahedr: Only 5 in 3D: tetrahedron, cube, octahedron, dodecahedron, icosahedron. (Plato discussed.)
missing.for_sphere_inscribed_in_cylinder_h_2r_: For sphere inscribed in cylinder (h = 2r): V_sphere = 2/3 × V_cylinder. (Archimedes's favorite result.)
missing.16_vertices_32_edges_24_faces_8_cells_: 16 vertices, 32 edges, 24 faces, 8 cells. Can be unfolded into 8 cubes (per Salvador Dalí).
missing.single_sided_surface_cut_down_middle_l: Single-sided surface. Cut down middle → longer single loop. Cut at 1/3 → two interlocked rings (one Möbius).
missing.closed_non_orientable_surface_inside_o: Closed non-orientable surface. Inside = outside. Can be made in 3D only with self-intersection.
missing.curve_of_constant_width_not_a_circle_c: Curve of constant width (not a circle!). Can roll a board on three Reuleaux rollers smoothly. Drill bits use it.
missing.two_parallel_polygons_rotated_360_2n_c: Two parallel polygons rotated 360°/2n, connected by triangles. Tetrahedron is the simplest antiprism.
missing.has_chirality_left_right_handed_forms_: Has chirality — left- + right-handed forms. One of two chiral Archimedean solids (other is snub dodecahedron).
missing.equation_z_xy_straight_lines_lie_on_it: Equation z = xy. Straight lines lie on it in 2 families. Used in Pringles + some roofs.
missing.gomboc_2006_is_first_known_mono_monost: "Gomboc" (2006) is first known mono-monostatic — one stable + one unstable equilibrium. Like a self-righting Weeble.
missing.cannot_tile_plane_alone_gaps_but_7_egg: Cannot tile plane alone — gaps. But 7 egg-shapes form a hex-like cluster.
missing.common_in_crystals_cube_nacl_octahedro: Common in crystals: cube (NaCl), octahedron (diamond, fluorite), dodecahedron (pyrite, garnet).
missing.truncated_icosahedron_60_carbons_like_: Truncated icosahedron. 60 carbons. Like soccer ball pattern. Discovered 1985.
missing.helical_10_5_base_pairs_per_turn_major: Helical, ~10.5 base pairs per turn. Major + minor grooves. Constant diameter ~2 nm.
missing.hexagonal_prisms_with_rhombic_bottoms_: Hexagonal prisms with rhombic bottoms. Bees do not consciously calculate — biophysics naturally minimizes surface area.
missing.drop_spaghetti_at_random_average_numbe: Drop spaghetti at random — average number of intersections = (L/πd) × π × n. Used to estimate π in classroom.
missing.study_of_flags_comes_from_latin_vexill: Study of flags. Comes from Latin vexillum (flag carried by Roman cavalry).
missing.keep_it_simple_a_child_can_draw_from_m: Keep it simple (a child can draw from memory), use meaningful symbolism, 2-3 basic colors, no lettering or seals, be distinctive.
missing.most_common_2_3_e_g_france_germany_us_: Most common: 2:3 (e.g., France, Germany). US is 1:1.9. UK is 1:2. Some unusual: Switzerland 1:1, Nepal non-rectangular.
missing.symbols_added_to_flag_stars_crosses_an: Symbols added to flag (stars, crosses, animals). Heraldic vocabulary describes positioning.
missing.upper_left_rectangle_about_1_4_the_fla: Upper-left rectangle (about 1/4 the flag). US stars go here. Australia, NZ also use canton design.
missing.hoist_side_attached_to_pole_fly_opposi: Hoist = side attached to pole. Fly = opposite (free) side.
missing.red_courage_revolution_blood_blue_ocea: Red = courage, revolution, blood. Blue = ocean, sky, freedom. Green = land, nature, Islam. White = peace, purity. Yellow/Gold = wealth, sun.
missing.flying_flag_upside_down_where_asymmetr: Flying flag upside-down (where asymmetric); flying flag at half-staff = mourning.
missing.light_blue_with_map_un_or_compass_nato: Light blue with map (UN) or compass (NATO). Intentionally avoid national/religious symbols.
missing.flags_are_codified_geometry_every_nati: Flags are codified geometry. Every national flag has precise specifications: aspect ratio, color codes, charge positions, star angles.
missing.x_12_means_x_inches_rise_per_12_inches: X:12 means X inches rise per 12 inches of horizontal run.
missing.149_597_870_700_m_defined_exactly_ligh: 149,597,870,700 m. Defined exactly. Light takes ~8.3 min to cross 1 AU.
missing.9_461_10_m_distance_light_travels_in_1: 9.461×10¹⁵ m. Distance light travels in 1 Julian year.
missing.3_26_light_years_distance_at_which_1_a: ~3.26 light-years. Distance at which 1 AU subtends 1 arcsecond.
missing.165_au_2024_fastest_human_made_object_: ~165 AU (2024). Fastest human-made object leaving solar system. Still receiving data.
missing.620_million_tons_of_h_he_every_second_: ~620 million tons of H → He every second. Loses ~4 million tons as energy (E = mc²).
missing.stream_of_charged_particles_400_800_km: Stream of charged particles. ~400-800 km/s near Earth. Creates auroras + sculpts comet tails.
missing.axial_tilt_drives_seasons_tilt_90_mean: Axial tilt drives seasons. Tilt > 90° means retrograde rotation (Venus, Uranus, Pluto).
missing.can_you_cross_each_of_the_7_bridges_of: Can you cross each of the 7 bridges of Königsberg exactly once?
missing.no_euler_proved_1736_you_need_each_nod: No. Euler proved (1736) you need each node to have even degree, except possibly 2 odd-degree nodes. Königsberg had 4 odd-degree nodes. Founded graph theory.
missing.using_only_compass_straightedge_constr: Using only compass + straightedge, construct a square with same area as a given circle.
missing.impossible_is_transcendental_lindemann: Impossible. π is transcendental (Lindemann 1882). Cannot be constructed with finite C+S operations.
missing.construct_a_cube_with_twice_the_volume: Construct a cube with twice the volume of a given cube using compass + straightedge.
missing.impossible_requires_2_which_is_algebra: Impossible. Requires ∛2, which is algebraic of degree 3, but C+S only produces 2ⁿ-degree numbers.
missing.divide_an_arbitrary_angle_into_3_equal: Divide an arbitrary angle into 3 equal parts with compass + straightedge.
missing.impossible_in_general_some_specific_an: Impossible in general. Some specific angles (e.g., 180°) can be. Origami CAN trisect arbitrary angles.
missing.given_two_circles_how_do_they_relate: Given two circles, how do they relate?
missing.5_cases_separate_externally_tangent_in: 5 cases: separate, externally tangent, intersecting (2 points), internally tangent, one inside the other.
missing.can_every_planar_map_be_colored_with_4: Can every planar map be colored with 4 colors so no adjacent regions share a color?
missing.yes_proven_by_appel_haken_1976_first_m: Yes. Proven by Appel + Haken (1976) — first major theorem proved with computer assistance.
missing.what_path_from_a_to_b_b_lower_minimize: What path from A to B (B lower) minimizes time of sliding object under gravity?
missing.cycloid_posed_by_bernoulli_1696_solved: Cycloid. Posed by Bernoulli (1696). Solved by Newton overnight + by 4 others including Leibniz.
missing.cut_a_circular_pizza_into_8_sectors_wi: Cut a circular pizza into 8 sectors with cuts through one point (not necessarily center). Alternating pieces have equal area.
missing.true_for_any_odd_multiple_of_4_sectors: True for any odd-multiple-of-4 sectors. Works for 8, 12, 16, ...
missing.for_what_radii_of_intersecting_circles: For what radii of intersecting circles do the moon-shape ("lune") + lens have equal area?
missing.ratio_depends_solved_analytically_roma: Ratio depends — solved analytically. Romantic geometry from a 1942 novel.
missing.intersection_of_two_perpendicular_cyli: Intersection of two perpendicular cylinders of same radius.
missing.has_4_vertices_looks_like_a_4_faced_cu: Has 4 vertices, looks like a 4-faced cushion. Volume = (16/3)r³.
missing.drop_a_needle_of_length_l_onto_paralle: Drop a needle of length L onto parallel lines spaced d apart. What's the probability it crosses a line?
missing.2l_d_for_l_d_method_to_compute_by_drop: 2L/(πd) for L ≤ d. Method to compute π by dropping needles!
missing.can_you_cut_a_circle_into_pieces_rearr: Can you cut a circle into pieces + rearrange them into a square of equal area?
missing.yes_laczkovich_1990_with_10_pieces_cho: Yes (Laczkovich 1990) — with ~10⁵⁰ pieces. Choice axiom required. Cannot use scissors-style cuts; pieces are bizarre.
missing.can_you_cut_a_ball_into_pieces_rearran: Can you cut a ball into pieces + rearrange them into two balls of the same size?
missing.yes_requires_axiom_of_choice_non_measu: Yes — requires axiom of choice + non-measurable pieces. Defies intuition but mathematically valid.
missing.why_do_soap_films_take_certain_shapes: Why do soap films take certain shapes?
missing.they_minimize_surface_area_catenoid_be: They minimize surface area. Catenoid (between rings), Plateau border (junctions). Yields beautiful geometry.
missing.3_hexagons_around_each_vertex_6_3_hone: 3 hexagons around each vertex. {6,3}. Honeycomb. Most efficient.
missing.octagons_squares_4_8_8_hispanic_coloni: Octagons + squares (4.8.8). Hispanic colonial tile patterns.
missing.just_rhombi_can_be_combined_to_make_pe: Just rhombi. Can be combined to make Penrose tilings.
missing.two_rhombi_fat_thin_aperiodic_never_re: Two rhombi (fat + thin). Aperiodic — never repeats. 5-fold symmetry.
missing.right_triangles_arranged_in_pinwheels_: Right triangles arranged in pinwheels. Aperiodic. Used in tiles at Federation Square (Melbourne).
missing.single_polygon_that_tiles_aperiodicall: Single polygon that tiles aperiodically. Major recent discovery by Smith, Myers, Kaplan, Goodman-Strauss.
missing.improvement_on_hat_uses_only_direct_no: Improvement on hat — uses only direct (not reflected) tiles.
missing.5_sided_tiles_found_in_cairo_street_pa: 5-sided tiles. Found in Cairo street paving. Each tile has 4 angles of ~120° + one of 90°.
missing.use_2_vertex_types_14_distinct_ones_so: Use 2 vertex types. 14 distinct ones (some debate). More complex than Archimedean.
missing.divide_plane_into_cells_closest_to_eac: Divide plane into cells closest to each "seed" point. Appears in nature (giraffe spots, foam).
missing.triangle_mesh_with_property_that_no_po: Triangle mesh with property that no point is inside any triangle's circumcircle. Dual of Voronoi.
missing.15_distinct_convex_pentagon_shapes_can: 15 distinct convex pentagon shapes can tile the plane. Last one found 2015 (Mann, McLoud, Von Derau). Marjorie Rice found 4 as amateur in 1970s.
missing.heptagons_impossible_in_euclidean_tile: Heptagons (impossible in Euclidean) tile hyperbolic plane with 3 around each vertex. Drawn by Escher.
missing.square_tiles_with_curves_or_diagonals_: Square tiles with curves or diagonals. Random orientation → maze-like patterns.
missing.latitude_longitude_in_decimal_degrees_: Latitude + longitude in decimal degrees. Lat: 0° = equator, ±90° = poles. Lon: 0° = Greenwich, ±180° = international date line area.
missing.at_each_vertex_you_need_3_polygons_who: At each vertex, you need 3+ polygons whose angle sum is < 360° (else flat or impossible). Only 5 combinations work for regular polygons.
missing.v_e_f_2_holds_for_every_convex_polyhed: V − E + F = 2 holds for every convex polyhedron (including all 5 Platonic solids).
missing.connecting_face_centers_of_a_platonic_: Connecting face centers of a Platonic solid gives its "dual". Cube ↔ octahedron; dodecahedron ↔ icosahedron; tetrahedron ↔ itself.
missing.13_semi_regular_solids_same_arrangemen: 13 semi-regular solids — same arrangement at each vertex, but multiple polygon types. Includes truncated icosahedron (soccer ball pattern).
missing.4_regular_star_polyhedra_with_pentagra: 4 regular star polyhedra with pentagram faces or vertices. Non-convex.
missing.start_with_triangle_on_each_side_repla: Start with triangle. On each side, replace middle third with two sides of a smaller triangle. Repeat. Infinite perimeter, finite area.
missing.start_with_triangle_remove_middle_smal: Start with triangle. Remove middle (smaller) triangle. Repeat on each remaining triangle. Hausdorff dimension log(3)/log(2) ≈ 1.585.
missing.remove_middle_third_of_segment_repeat_: Remove middle third of segment. Repeat on each remaining segment. Uncountably infinite but total length zero.
missing.points_c_where_iterating_z_z_c_stays_b: Points c where iterating z = z² + c stays bounded. Infinite detail at any zoom. Discovered 1980.
missing.for_each_c_points_z_where_iterating_z_: For each c, points z where iterating z = z² + c stays bounded. Each c gives a different Julia set; together they tile the Mandelbrot set.
missing.fold_a_strip_of_paper_in_half_repeated: Fold a strip of paper in half repeatedly; unfold each fold at 90°. Or recursive replacement of segments.
missing.iterated_function_system_random_select: Iterated function system. Random selection of 4 affine maps produces a fern shape.
missing.fractional_dimension_coastline_1_2_1_3: Fractional dimension. Coastline ~1.2-1.3 depending on country. Captures "roughness" beyond integer dimensions.
missing.defining_property_strict_self_similari: Defining property. Strict self-similarity (Koch) or statistical (coastlines).
missing.coastlines_trees_lungs_bronchi_blood_v: Coastlines, trees, lungs (bronchi), blood vessels, river networks, mountain ranges, clouds, lightning.
missing.flat_plane_parallel_postulate_holds_an: Flat plane. Parallel postulate holds. Angles in triangle sum to exactly 180°.
missing.surface_of_sphere_triangle_angles_sum_: Surface of sphere. Triangle angles sum to MORE than 180°. Excess proportional to area. Used in navigation.
missing.saddle_shaped_surface_triangle_angles_: Saddle-shaped surface. Triangle angles sum to LESS than 180°. Many parallels through a point not on a line.
missing.shortest_paths_on_a_surface_straight_l: Shortest paths on a surface. Straight lines (Euclidean); great circles (sphere); hyperbolic lines.
missing.largest_circles_on_a_sphere_equator_me: Largest circles on a sphere. Equator + meridians. Plane flights follow great-circle paths.
missing.euclidean_0_spherical_positive_hyperbo: Euclidean = 0. Spherical = positive. Hyperbolic = negative. Curvature determines geometry.
missing.on_sphere_of_radius_r_triangle_area_r_: On sphere of radius R, triangle area = R² × (angle sum − π).
missing.positions_on_earth_s_surface_use_spher: Positions on Earth's surface use spherical (or more precisely, ellipsoidal) geometry.
missing.nyc_to_tokyo_is_shortest_via_the_arcti: NYC to Tokyo is shortest via the Arctic, not straight east — that's the great circle.
missing.spacetime_is_curved_by_mass_energy_geo: Spacetime is curved by mass-energy. Geodesics through curved spacetime ARE the paths of free-falling objects.
missing.a_vertical_or_angled_gnomon_casts_a_sh: A vertical or angled "gnomon" casts a shadow. As Earth rotates, shadow moves across markings.
missing.for_accuracy_gnomon_must_be_parallel_t: For accuracy, gnomon must be parallel to Earth's axis — angle to horizontal = local latitude.
missing.dial_plate_perpendicular_to_gnomon_par: Dial plate perpendicular to gnomon (parallel to equator). Hour lines evenly spaced 15° apart.
missing.dial_plate_parallel_to_ground_hour_lin: Dial plate parallel to ground. Hour lines NOT evenly spaced — formula: tan(hour line angle) = sin(latitude) × tan(hour angle).
missing.dial_on_a_wall_hour_lines_depend_on_wa: Dial on a wall. Hour lines depend on wall's azimuth + latitude.
missing.sundial_time_differs_from_clock_time_b: Sundial time differs from clock time by up to ~16 minutes through the year. Due to Earth's elliptical orbit + axial tilt.
missing.figure_8_trace_of_sun_s_position_at_sa: Figure-8 trace of sun's position at same clock time each day for a year. Shown on some advanced sundials.
missing.sundial_shows_local_apparent_solar_tim: Sundial shows LOCAL apparent solar time. Differs from civil time by longitude offset within timezone + DST.
missing.very_precise_sundials_with_corrections: Very precise sundials with corrections for equation of time + longitude. Accurate to seconds.
missing.phi_1_61803398874989_irrational_roots_: φ (phi) ≈ 1.61803398874989... Irrational. Roots of x² = x + 1.
missing.a_line_divided_so_that_whole_larger_la: A line divided so that whole : larger = larger : smaller. (a+b)/a = a/b = φ.
missing.ratio_of_consecutive_fibonacci_numbers: Ratio of consecutive Fibonacci numbers approaches φ: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21 → 21/13 ≈ 1.615.
missing.long_side_short_side_remove_a_square_r: Long side / short side = φ. Remove a square → remaining rectangle is also golden.
missing.logarithmic_spiral_with_growth_factor_: Logarithmic spiral with growth factor φ per quarter turn. Approximated by Fibonacci squares + arcs.
missing.ratios_of_pentagram_diagonals_to_sides: Ratios of pentagram diagonals to sides = φ. Pythagoras knew this.
missing.spiral_arrangements_in_sunflowers_pine: Spiral arrangements in sunflowers, pinecones, nautilus shells. Reflects optimal packing for growing meristems.
missing.often_claimed_in_parthenon_mona_lisa_e: Often claimed in Parthenon, Mona Lisa, etc. — most claims unsupported. Real golden ratio art: 20th-century deliberate use (Le Corbusier, Dali).
missing.long_believed_humans_find_golden_ratio: Long believed humans find golden ratio most beautiful. Robust evidence weaker than popular accounts suggest.
missing.1_1_1_1_1_1_most_irrational_number_slo: φ = 1 + 1/(1 + 1/(1 + 1/(...))). Most irrational number — slowest converging continued fraction.
missing.active_research_area_can_solve_cubic_e: Active research area. Can solve cubic equations (impossible with compass + straightedge alone). Connect to algebra.
missing.at_any_flat_foldable_vertex_mountain_f: At any flat-foldable vertex, |# mountain folds − # valley folds| = 2.
missing.at_a_flat_foldable_vertex_alternating_: At a flat-foldable vertex, alternating angles around the vertex sum to 180°.
missing.standard_paper_can_be_folded_in_half_7: Standard paper can be folded in half ~7 times. Each fold doubles thickness. After 7, paper is too stiff. Record (with special equipment + huge paper): 12 folds.
missing.flat_sheet_can_fold_into_virtually_any: Flat sheet can fold into virtually any 3D shape with enough creases. Modern origami extremely complex.
missing.james_webb_space_telescope_sunshield_f: James Webb Space Telescope sunshield folded for launch (origami principles), unfolded in space.
missing.folded_efficiently_to_fit_in_compartme: Folded efficiently to fit in compartment, unfold rapidly.
missing.origami_inspired_medical_stents_fold_t: Origami-inspired medical stents fold tiny for insertion, expand in artery.
missing.parallelogram_based_fold_lets_large_su: Parallelogram-based fold lets large surface (solar panel) deploy from compact bundle via single pull.
missing.cuts_allowed_in_addition_to_folds_used: Cuts allowed in addition to folds. Used for pop-up books + scientific structures.
missing.parallel_lines_converge_to_vanishing_p: Parallel lines converge to vanishing points. 1-point (looking down a road), 2-point (corner of building), 3-point (looking up at a tower).
missing.where_parallel_lines_appear_to_meet_on: Where parallel lines appear to meet on horizon. Determined by viewer's position + line direction.
missing.horizontal_line_at_viewer_s_eye_height: Horizontal line at viewer's eye height. All vanishing points lie on it (for lines parallel to ground).
missing.objects_pointing_at_viewer_appear_comp: Objects pointing at viewer appear compressed. Drawing a hand pointed at viewer = challenge.
missing.divide_canvas_photo_into_9_equal_secti: Divide canvas/photo into 9 equal sections. Place subjects on intersections for dynamic composition.
missing.camera_tilted_off_horizontal_creates_u: Camera tilted off-horizontal creates unease, instability, dynamism.
missing.sun_angle_determines_shadow_length_dir: Sun angle determines shadow length + direction. Low angle (golden hour) = long shadows + warm color.
missing.brunelleschi_1413_demonstrated_mathema: Brunelleschi (~1413) demonstrated mathematical linear perspective. Alberti formalized it (1435).
missing.distant_objects_appear_bluer_less_dist: Distant objects appear bluer + less distinct due to atmospheric scattering.
missing.some_traditions_use_specific_angles_ra: Some traditions use specific angles + ratios (Islamic geometric patterns, Indian mandalas, gothic cathedrals).
missing.mathematical_artist_impossible_objects: Mathematical artist. Impossible objects (Penrose triangles), tessellations, hyperbolic geometry.
missing.folding_angles_crease_patterns_produce: Folding angles + crease patterns produce 3D sculptures from flat paper.
missing.curves_formed_when_a_plane_intersects_: Curves formed when a plane intersects a (double) cone. The angle of the plane to the cone's axis determines the curve type.
missing.the_only_5_convex_regular_polyhedra_al: The only 5 convex regular polyhedra. All faces are congruent regular polygons, same number meeting at each vertex.
missing.shapes_that_exhibit_self_similarity_at: Shapes that exhibit self-similarity at every scale. Coined by Benoit Mandelbrot (1975) from Latin fractus, "broken".
missing.geometries_on_curved_surfaces_euclid_s: Geometries on curved surfaces. Euclid's parallel postulate doesn't hold. Discovered 1820s (Lobachevsky, Bolyai, Gauss).
missing.projecting_curved_earth_onto_a_flat_ma: Projecting curved Earth onto a flat map ALWAYS distorts something — angles, areas, distances, or shapes. Each projection optimizes for different uses.
missing.many_popular_claims_about_the_golden_r: Many popular claims about the golden ratio in art, architecture, and "beauty" are unsupported or invented retroactively. The mathematical properties are real + beautiful; the cultural claims often aren't.
missing.origami_connects_geometry_to_engineeri: Origami connects geometry to engineering, biology, and design. Mathematical theorems govern what folds are possible.
missing.many_athletic_skills_depend_on_launch_: Many athletic skills depend on launch angles. The classic "45° is optimal" is true only for projectiles launched + landing at the same height (without air resistance).
